title: The Wrong Way Home by Edward Taylor — Love Poetry Lines
content:
All night a door floated down the river.
It tried to remember little incidents of pleasure
from its former life, like the time the lovers
leaned against it kissing for hours
and whispering those famous words.
Later, there were harsh words and a shoe
was thrown and the door was slammed.
Comings and goings by the thousands,
meaning: "The Wrong Way Home" by Edward Taylor is a love and deep and nature poem consisting of 17 lines. The poet explores themes of love and deep and nature through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "All night a door floated down the river. It tried to remember little incidents of pleasure...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Edward Taylor's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
